notes: Logan: RAMP-C 'one of the cheapest tricks IBM could have pulled'
Logan: 'A benchmark should be something that is widely circulated and can be duplicated and verified'
First WSJ feature naming Aberdeen Group with Logan affiliation; 5+ months before official Dec 1988 founding
DEC sent competitive-analysis 'truth squads' to influencers ahead of AS/400 launch
DEC alleges VAX comm lines were set at 1% the speed of IBM's
DEC says typical customer uses 10x faster network than RAMP-C tested
Weiss/Gartner: vendor-benchmark fight 'indicates a degree of desperation in this marketplace'
IBM declined to release RAMP-C software or allow independent auditors to verify
Unnamed analyst (fearing IBM retaliation) said 'questionable aspects' in IBM tests
TPC founded August 1988 — 2 months after this article
